title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: basic example shows solve common problem: special using README.Rmd instead just README.md? can include R chunks like : ’ll still need render README.Rmd regularly, keep README.md --date. devtools::build_readme() handy . also use GitHub Actions re-render README.Rmd every time push. example workflow can found : https://github.com/r-lib/actions/tree/v1/examples. can also embed plots, example:  case, don’t forget commit push resulting figure files, display GitHub CRAN.
code: library(afpca) ## basic example code summary(cars) #>      speed           dist        #>  Min.   : 4.0   Min.   :  2.00   #>  1st Qu.:12.0   1st Qu.: 26.00   #>  Median :15.0   Median : 36.00   #>  Mean   :15.4   Mean   : 42.98   #>  3rd Qu.:19.0   3rd Qu.: 56.00   #>  Max.   :25.0   Max.   :120.00

what: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Decomposes functional observations using adaptive functional principal component analysis. method incorporates adaptive smoothness estimated decomposition using penalized likelihood framework

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: fpca.adapt(   data,   basis = "trunc.poly",   poly.degree = 2,   knots = NA,   nbs = 35,   n.comp = 18,   pve = 0.99,   normalize.scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_fpcs = TRUE,   ntimes = 100 )

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: data data frame, matrix functional observations. basis Type spline basis poly.degree degree spline basis knots Number knots used generate spline basis estimate mean function functional principal components nbs Number spline basis generate mean function functional principal components n.comp prespecified number principal components pve proportion variance explained; used choose number functional principal components normalize.scores normalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_scores orthogonalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_fpcs orthogonalize FPCs iteration (default true, recommended) ntimes Maximum number iterations
